summary: AI workflow automation for distributor orders, quotes, invoices, purchasing, and ERP data.
editor_note: The bet is that distributors do not need another ERP module; they need agents inside the inbox-to-warehouse gap. Canals names the ugly work directly: sales-order intake, quote generation, invoice processing, purchasing, customer service, and structured ERP handoff, with 100+ distributor partners cited. Unlike generic RPA, it starts with wholesale documents and supplier workflows. The risk is whether ERP edge cases pull it back into services.

product_evidence.claim: Official material positions Canals as AI workflow automation for industrial supply-chain transactions across distributors, manufacturers, and contractors.
Funding coverage says the workflow suite handles sales order entry, quote generation, invoice processing, purchasing, and customer service for distributors.

market_context.primary_user: Wholesale distributors and industrial suppliers with email, PDF, spreadsheet, portal, and ERP handoff work.
market_context.differentiation: Vertical workflow agents for distributor transaction documents instead of a horizontal RPA layer.
market_context.why_now: The May 28, 2026 Series A gives Canals capital to expand its AI workflow suite for wholesale distribution.

risks.claim: The main risk is whether ERP and supplier edge cases require heavy services work that slows software margins.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product and funding-source review.
